summary: Chromium browser credential stealer packaged as bytenode bytecode with native debug-attach addon
details: @ibrahim1337/baksen ships a credential-stealing toolkit targeting Chromium-family browsers (Chrome, Brave, Edge) on Windows. The package's main entry point loads bytenode-compiled bytecode (index.jsc) which: (1) enumerates Chrome/Brave/Edge user profiles, (2) terminates running browser processes via taskkill, (3) invokes a bundled 676KB Windows native addon at build/Release/debugelevator.node that attaches a debug session to browser processes to extract the App-Bound encryption master key (Chrome v20 encryption), (4) decrypts the browser Login Data (SELECT origin_url, username_value, password_value FROM logins) and Cookies SQLite stores, and (5) writes the decrypted passwords and cookies to plaintext files in the current working directory. The main JavaScript source is distributed only as V8 bytecode (.jsc) via bytenode, with js-confuser declared as a devDependency, concealing the credential-theft logic from normal source review. Package metadata is placeholder (description is just 'baksen'; repository, bugs, and homepage all point to github.com/yourusername/baksen), and the README does not document any of the browser-debugging or credential-extraction behavior. Installing or loading this package on a Windows machine with Chromium-family browsers results in theft of saved passwords and live session cookies.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
